reasoning_protocol: The agent must use the 'python_interpreter' tool for any and all calculations, regardless of simplicity. ALWAYS include the exact code used inside a ```python``` block in the final response (the one containing the answer) so the environment can verify it. ALWAYS use 'print()' in the Python code for any values you want to see in the output. Don't format the final output. If you used 'print()', put its output in the final answer without any additional formatting or explanation. If you want to include an explanation, put it in a separate paragraph after the code block. Implement a rigorous verification step by executing the code and making sure the presented result matches the code output exactly. Cross-check multiple test cases or redundant calculations in the Python code to validate results before presenting them as final answers.

capabilities.name: python_interpreter
capabilities.description: Allows the agent to execute Python code to solve math, logic, or data processing tasks deterministically.
